revision.text:
[[ja:庵寺山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Anderayama Kofun (庵寺山古墳) | image = | caption = | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Northern Cluster/Hirono Subgroup) | location = {{ill|Uji|qid=Q326956|1=Uji}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34|52|29.73|N|135|47|20.68|E|display=inline,title|qq=}} | type = {{ill|Circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter: 56 m
Height: 9 m | burial = {{ill|Clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} (containing a box-shaped wooden coffin) | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, weapons, farming and fishing tools, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} | satellite_tombs = | built = Late 4th century to early 5th century | Interred_individual = Unknown | tomb = | notes = Uji City Designated Historic Site (庵寺山古墳) | tangible_notes = | special_notes = | map = Japan Kyoto | type = Circular mound | map_dot_label = Anderayama Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Anderayama Kofun''' (庵寺山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in Maruyama, Hirono-cho, {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|qid=Q326956|lt=Uji|1=Uji}}, Kyoto Prefecture. It is a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, specifically the Northern Cluster or Hirono Subgroup. The site is Historic Site1 as a Uji City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:庵寺山古墳出土 蓋形埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Lid-shaped haniwa|qq=}}{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|en|Kyoto University Museum|qid=Q11374929|lt=Kyoto University Museum|1=Kyoto University Museum}}|qq=}}]] [[File:庵寺山古墳出土 靫形埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Quiver-shaped haniwa|qq=}}{{small|Exhibited at Kyoto University Museum.|qq=}}]] Located at the western edge of a spur extending from the Uji Hills (elevation: 65 meters), Anderayama Kofun is a large circular Kofun. It types part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, along with nearby mounds such as Konpira-yama Kofun, Bōzu-yama Kofun Cluster, Ipponmatsu Kofun, and Uji Maruyama Kofun. The name derives from the nearby Kumaore-an temple, which was abandoned in the late Edo period.{{Harvid|Uji City Archaeological Report Vol. 15|1990|qq=}} Residential development in the mid-1970s resulted in partial destruction of the mound and its surrounding area, though multiple investigations have since been conducted. The mound has a diameter of 56 meters and a height of 9 meters.{{Harvid|Anderayama Kofun (Revised)|2002|qq=}} Due to the lack of investigation on the slopes, it remains unclear whether terracing or {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|qid=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} was employed.{{Harvid|Uji City Archaeological Report Vol. 15|1990|qq=}} A rectangular arrangement of haniwa was identified at the mound's summit, including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} with fins and various {{ill|figured haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figured haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}}, such as house-shaped, lid-shaped, quiver-shaped, and armor-shaped haniwa.{{Harvid|Uji City Archaeological Report Vol. 15|1990|qq=}} The lid- and quiver-shaped haniwa are particularly noteworthy for their size and craftsmanship.{{Harvid|Uji City Archaeological Report Vol. 15|1990|qq=}} The burial facility, a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}, was located at the summit. It housed a box-shaped wooden coffin and measured 11.7 meters north-south and 5.2 meters east-west.{{Harvid|Uji City Archaeological Report Vol. 15|1990|qq=}} The coffin itself measured 7 meters in length and 0.8 meters in width.{{Harvid|Anderayama Kofun (Revised)|2002|qq=}} Despite large-map_size looting, burial goods such as bronze mirrors, weapons, and farming and fishing tools were recovered. The mound is dated to the late 4th century to early 5th century, marking the early {{ill|Kofun period|lt=middleKofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Harvid|Anderayama Kofun (Revised)|2002|qq=}} The site was Historic Site1 a Uji City Historic Site in 1997. == Archaeological History == * Late Edo period: Southern half of the summit flattened, with a grave installed.{{Harvid|Uji City Archaeological Report Vol. 15|1990|qq=}} * 1944: Investigation of exposed haniwa by Kyoto University (results unpublished; burial goods stored at Kyoto University Museum). * Late 1960s: Final looting activity.{{Harvid|Uji City Archaeological Report Vol. 15|1990|qq=}} * 1973: Survey and measurement by Kyoto Prefectural Jonan High School (now Kyoto Prefectural Jonan Risho High School); mound reported as 26 meters in diameter.{{Harvid|Anderayama Kofun (Revised)|2002|qq=}} * 1975: Investigation during residential development; confirmed as the largest circular mound in southern Yamashiro.{{Harvid|Uji City Archaeological Report Vol. 15|1990|qq=}} * 1989: Investigation of haniwa arrangements and looting pits by Uji City Board of Education.{{Harvid|Uji City Archaeological Report Vol. 15|1990|qq=}} * 1996: Investigation of mound and burial facility by Uji City Board of Education.{{Harvid|Anderayama Kofun (Revised)|2002|qq=}} * March 25, 1997: Designated as a Uji City Historic Site. == burial goods == burial goods recovered from the clay coffin include:{{Harvid|Anderayama Kofun (Revised)|2002|qq=}} ; Inside the coffin: * One paired-animal motif mirror (仿製対置式神獣鏡) ; Outside the coffin: * Nine iron swords * Two iron daggers * Three hoes * Seven sickles * Eighteen harvesting tools * Five axes * Three chisels * Two awls * Three adzes * Eleven knives * One unidentified tool * Three fishhooks * One spearhead The farming and fishing tools were discovered on the southern end of the clay coffin, sealed under a clay layer.{{Harvid|Anderayama Kofun (Revised)|2002|qq=}} == Cultural Property Designation == === Uji City Cultural Property === * Historic Site: ** Anderayama Kofun – Designated March 25, 1997. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|en|Kyoto University Museum|qid=Q11374929|lt=Kyoto University Museum|1=Kyoto University Museum}} (Kyoto City, Sakyo Ward) – Houses and displays haniwa from Anderayama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Anderayama Kofun Excavation Report, Vol. 15|editor=Uji City Board of Education|year=1990|chapter=Summary of the 1989 Investigation|ref={{Harvid|Uji City Archaeological Report Vol. 15|1990|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Cite book|title=Revised Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun|editor=Arakawa Fumishi|year=2002|ref={{Harvid|Anderayama Kofun (Revised)|2002|qq=}}|qq=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|庵寺山古墳|version=104577327|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:庵寺山古墳]] {{Commons category|Anderayama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q130455352}} [[Category:久津川古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:京都府内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:History of Uji, Kyoto]]
